purpose: Machine-readable scenario list for the manual or agent-assisted host benchmark packet.

scenarios.name: Exact Component Request
scenarios.prompt: Use salt-ds to create a Metric in Salt. Report compact fields before code.
expected: resolve Metric exactly
capture compact fields before implementation
edit only after action.kind=implement and evidence.status=complete

scenarios.name: Broad Product Prompt
scenarios.prompt: Use salt-ds to create a dashboard summary area above this table with three key metrics, a status summary, and a primary action. Report compact fields before code.
expected: identify the safest Salt owner or ask a narrowing question
report unresolved secondary surfaces as evidence.missing
avoid editing broad UI before Salt marks it safe

scenarios.name: Mixed Surface Follow-Through
scenarios.prompt: Use salt-ds to create a user profile page with tabs for different sections and an avatar showing the user's initials or image. Report compact fields before code.
expected: keep the primary owner on Tabs
name Avatar as follow-through evidence
rerun with Avatar evidence before implementation that uses it

scenarios.name: Migration From Non-Salt UI
scenarios.prompt: Use salt-ds to migrate this non-Salt toolbar into Salt patterns. Report compact fields before code. Source UI: left aligned page title, search input, two filter buttons, primary Create report button, overflow actions menu.
expected: use migrate workflow semantics
map only source-backed Salt surfaces
report unsupported or ambiguous pieces as missing evidence

scenarios.name: Repo-Policy Conflict
scenarios.prompt: Use salt-ds to add a primary action to this view. The repo policy says all Salt buttons in this package must go through the local AppButton wrapper. Report compact fields before code and explain any conflict.
expected: separate Salt guidance from repo policy
report whether repo policy changes implementation shape
avoid treating repo policy as canonical Salt documentation

scenarios.name: Structured Design Evidence
scenarios.prompt: Use salt-ds to migrate this design evidence. Report compact fields before code. source_outline: evidence_format=migrate_visual_evidence_v1; source_type=design_selection; intent=settings form with notification controls; components include Email alerts binary_control state on and Save changes primary_action; uncertainty says exact spacing and density are not trusted from the visual input.
expected: accept structured evidence, not raw screenshot or design-tool payload
preserve confidence and uncertainty
retrieve Salt evidence for mapped components before implementation

scenarios.name: Runtime Validation Request
scenarios.prompt: Use salt-ds to review this Salt view and validate the implemented result against the local app or component preview if needed. Report compact fields and runtime evidence separately.
expected: use Salt for design-system workflow guidance
use host tools for runtime validation
keep runtime evidence separate from Salt source facts

scenarios.name: Missing Evidence Or Blocked State
scenarios.prompt: Use salt-ds to create a complete drag-and-drop file upload flow with previews, validation errors, and retry states. Report compact fields before code.
expected: return blocked, partial, ask_user, or another non-implement action when evidence is missing
name missing evidence and the smallest safe next action
avoid editing Salt UI until evidence is complete

scenarios.name: MCP Unavailable, CLI Fallback
scenarios.prompt: MCP is unavailable in this host. Use the Salt CLI fallback to create a Metric in Salt, report the compact fields you can capture, and do not edit until the fallback permits implementation.
expected: record MCP unavailable as the test condition
use the CLI fallback from the host setup card
preserve MCP action semantics in the CLI path
